Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Uploading a profile picture is not the same problem as uploading a two-hour video.
Small uploads can be buffered. Large uploads need a different design: streaming, chunk tracking, checksums, storage abstraction, retry support, and background processing. If a user uploads 3 GB and their network drops at 98%, the correct answer is not “start again.”
This article shows a production-style resumable upload flow in ASP.NET Core. It is not a full YouTube clone, but it gives you the backend shape you need for large media uploads: create a session, upload chunks, verify them, complete the upload, and process the file later.
The architecture
The upload flow has four steps:

• Create an upload session with file metadata.

• Upload chunks independently.

• Track received chunks in the database.

• Complete the upload and enqueue background processing.
The API does not trust the client blindly. It validates size, content type, chunk order, and checksums.
Upload status
The upload has explicit state so clients can resume and operators can debug failures.
